rationale: Both candidates independently arrive at a variant of the ground-truth's core theme (dsocek's 'derive from existing metadata instead of hardcoding'), though neither cites the actual _packed_modules_mapping/stacked_params_mapping fused-projection concern, so recall is similarly partial for both. X's second finding (text-encoder LoRA still a silent no-op) is a well-grounded, novel extension of the PR's own stated bug pattern, while Y's second finding (flux2_klein/ovis_image 'unintended scanning') is self-undermining — its own evidence shows only docstring mentions of self.unet, not real attributes, so the claimed risk likely doesn't exist. Y also outputs triplicated identical review_text blocks (a formatting/process artifact) and lands on 'REQUEST CHANGES' for what are admittedly minor suggestions, mismatching the actual outcome (two LGTM approvals, merged) — X's 'COMMENT'-level verdict is better calibrated to reality and its comments are more actionable and precise.
